close_reason: Subsumed by .6 (chunking is related to max buffer size)
description: SecretStore::get() (lib.rs:219-222) issues a single nv_read() call for the entire data_size. The TPM2 spec limits each TPM2_NV_Read command to TPM2_MAX_NV_BUFFER_SIZE bytes (typically 1024-2048 depending on the TPM). If an NV index holds more data than the TPM's NV_BUFFER_MAX, the single read fails with a TPM error (TPM_RC_NV_RANGE or similar). The tss-esapi library provides a chunked reader (NvOpenOptions/NvReaderWriter in abstraction::nv) but this code doesn't use it. The same issue applies to nv_write() in WritableSecretStore::put(). For NV indices storing small secrets (API keys, tokens) this rarely triggers, but the code silently fails for legitimate NV sizes above ~1024 bytes.
title: NV read does not chunk large reads
